official_sources.title: REST API endpoints for workflow runs - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs with status/branch/per_page/page filters (status accepts both statuses and conclusions) returning {total_count, workflow_runs}; GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id} with status and conclusion (null until terminal; success/failure/cancelled/timed_out/neutral/skipped/action_required/stale); POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}/rerun-failed-jobs with optional enable_debug_logging (default false) answering 201 Created with no example body

official_sources.title: Using pagination in the REST API - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: the Link response header carries <url>; rel="next"/"prev"/"first"/"last"; use the URLs from the link header to request the next page rather than constructing them; missing rel="next" marks the last page; per_page propagates into the link URLs

official_sources.title: API versions - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: X-GitHub-Api-Version header; supported versions 2026-03-10 (latest) and 2022-11-28 (default when the header is omitted); unsupported versions receive 410 Gone

research.language_rationale: The CI maintenance tooling this monitor extends is a Python codebase (the existing ghactions client ships in this repo), and GitHub documents workflow-run listing, rerun-failed-jobs, and Link-header pagination as plain REST — Python's stdlib urllib/json/http.server exercises the documented surface hermetically with zero dependencies, so raw REST is the credible integration path.

verified_facts: Workflow runs list at GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs with status/branch/per_page/page query filters and return {total_count, workflow_runs}; the status filter accepts conclusion values such as failure.

Re-running only the failed jobs of a run is its own documented operation: POST .../actions/runs/{run_id}/rerun-failed-jobs with optional enable_debug_logging, answering 201 Created with an empty body (the docs list the status code and no response payload) — this corrects the catalog's stale claim of a 202.
A workflow run is terminal when status is completed; conclusion stays null before that and then reports success/failure/cancelled/timed_out/neutral/skipped/action_required/stale.

REST pagination is driven by the Link response header (rel next/prev/first/last); clients follow the server-issued URLs — which can be canonical /repositories/{id} forms — instead of rebuilding them, and stop when rel="next" disappears.
Current REST calls send Accept: application/vnd.github+json plus X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2026-03-10 and a real User-Agent; errors arrive as {message, documentation_url}.
